message:
feat(stdlib): add pidfile_* family (FreeBSD libutil) (bd-7hs5s)

FreeBSD/libbsd PID-file management for daemons. The five functions
implement the standard "open + lock + write pid + clean unlink"
lifecycle used by sshd, mysqld, postgres, ntpd, and basically
every BSD/Linux daemon. Currently missing entirely from
frankenlibc-abi.

* pidfile_open(path, mode, *otherpid) atomically opens or creates
  the file and acquires an exclusive non-blocking flock via the
  recently-shipped flopen(O_CREAT|O_RDWR|O_EXLOCK|O_NONBLOCK).
  On lock contention returns NULL with errno=EEXIST and writes
  the existing locker's pid to *otherpid (parsed from the file
  contents; -1 on parse failure). mode defaults to 0o600 when 0.
* pidfile_write(pfh) ftruncate's then writes the current pid as
  decimal ASCII + trailing newline.
* pidfile_close(pfh) releases the lock without unlinking — used
  in double-fork patterns where the child takes over.
* pidfile_remove(pfh) unlinks then closes (standard cleanup).
* pidfile_fileno(pfh) returns the underlying fd.

Opaque PidFh struct is malloc'd by pidfile_open with fd + a
malloc'd copy of the path so pidfile_remove can unlink without
re-resolving.

9 abi integration tests on /tmp: create+lock+remove cycle, write
+ read-back pid match, contention surfaces EEXIST + otherpid,
close-without-unlink lets re-acquire succeed, NULL guards on all
five entrypoints, write overwrites longer-than-pid file content
without leaving trailing junk. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All
builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
